description: Following a series of high-profile violent incidents, the Japanese government pivoted its mental health policy regarding 'hikikomori' (social recluses). The debate centered on whether to implement compulsory state-led intervention programs versus the traditional approach of leaving intervention to families, fundamentally altering the social contract on mental health privacy.

key_events.description: Kawasaki stabbings occur, dramatically increasing public fear.
Cabinet Office releases data estimating over 1 million people are in social withdrawal.
Ministry of Health, Labour and Welfare mandates local municipalities to establish outreach support.

calibration_notes: Models how a sudden 'trigger event' can shift public opinion to support more intrusive state intervention in mental health.
